source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Lina has placed two shapes on a $5 \times 5$ board, as shown in the picture on the right. Which of the following five shapes should she place on the empty part of the board so that none of the remaining four shapes will fit in the empty space that is left? (The shapes may be rotated or turned over, but can only be placed so that they cover complete squares.)
<image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: 选项字段仅提供字母标签，具体五个形状内容依赖题图。
题干中含有 <image1>、<image2> 占位符，记录中实际图片路径为 image 字段。

image_dependency: Question explicitly says the board and shapes are 'shown in the picture on the right'
Solving depends on visual shape layout, empty-space geometry, and allowed rotations/reflections
multi_step: Need to infer the empty region left on the 5x5 board after the two placed shapes
Need to test candidate placements among five shapes under rotation or turning over
Need an additional exclusion step: choose the shape so that none of the other four shapes can fit the remaining space
This is more than direct recognition; it requires sequential spatial reasoning and comparison across options
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice with a single labeled answer ('D')
Constraints are crisp and checkable: complete-square coverage only, rotations/reflections allowed, fit/non-fit conditions are objective
There are only five finite candidates, so exhaustive verification is plausible once the image shapes are extracted

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, answer, and the referenced image are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded issues.
The sample has strong candidate quality signals with high image_dependency_score (0.97), multi_step_score (0.81), and verifiability_score (0.84).
Although solving requires visual parsing and shape extraction from the image, the necessary image asset exists, so the core semantics appear recoverable and usable.
The answer is only an option label, which adds some downstream resolution risk, but this does not outweigh the otherwise complete and high-value sample.
